title: Longest Valid Parentheses
prompt: Given a string containing just the characters `'('` and `')'`, return the length of the longest valid (well-formed) parentheses substring.

examples.output:
2
Explanation: The longest valid parentheses substring is "()".

4
Explanation: The longest valid parentheses substring is "()()".

notesForAI.coreIdea: Stack-based: push indices onto a stack. Initialize with -1 as a base. For '(', push index. For ')', pop; if stack empty push current index as new base, else result = max(result, i - stack.top()). Alternatively, use DP where dp[i] = length of longest valid substring ending at i.
commonBugs: Forgetting the initial -1 sentinel on the stack
Not considering concatenated valid substrings like ()() which should return 4
DP transition missing the case where the character before the matched substring is also '('
Returning the count of matched pairs instead of the length
notesForAI.expectedComplexity: O(n) time, O(n) space with stack; O(1) space with two-pass counting
